# niche-dashboard — Full Details

**Parent:** `niche-dashboard/SKILL.md`

**Auto-generated by Context Diet:** 2026-03-13

**This file contains the full specification. SKILL.md is the routing card.**


## Pattern 1: Feed Engine (Data Layer)

**What it does:** Fetches RSS feeds through a server-side proxy, parses XML, caches results, handles failures gracefully.

**How World Monitor does it:**
- Feeds defined in `src/config/feeds.ts` — each feed has: name, URL (proxied), category, tier (1-4 authority ranking)
- RSS proxy: client calls `/api/rss-proxy?url=<encoded-feed-url>` — Vercel serverless function fetches the feed server-side (avoids CORS)
- In-memory cache with 30min TTL + IndexedDB persistent cache for offline
- Feed failure tracking: after 2 failures, feed enters 5-minute cooldown (no retry spam)
- Feed scoping by language: `feedName::lang` keys for multi-locale support

**How to build yours:**

```typescript
// config/feeds.ts — Define your niche feeds
export interface Feed {
  name: string;
  url: string;          // RSS feed URL
  category: string;     // Panel it belongs to
  tier: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4; // 1=official source, 4=blog
}

// Example: Solar Industry
export const FEEDS: Feed[] = [
  { name: 'Solar Power World', url: 'https://www.solarpowerworldonline.com/feed/', category: 'industry-news', tier: 2 },
  { name: 'PV Magazine', url: 'https://www.pv-magazine.com/feed/', category: 'industry-news', tier: 2 },
  { name: 'SEIA', url: 'https://www.seia.org/feed', category: 'policy', tier: 1 },
  { name: 'EIA Solar', url: 'https://www.eia.gov/rss/todayinenergy.xml', category: 'data', tier: 1 },
  { name: 'r/solar', url: 'https://www.reddit.com/r/solar/.rss', category: 'community', tier: 4 },
];
```

```typescript
// api/rss-proxy.ts — Vercel serverless RSS proxy (avoids CORS)
export default async function handler(req: Request) {
  const url = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get('url');
  if (!url) return new Response('Missing url param', { status: 400 });

  // Allowlist check (prevent SSRF)
  const allowed = FEEDS.some(f => url.startsWith(new URL(f.url).origin));
  if (!allowed) return new Response('Domain not allowed', { status: 403 });

  const response = await fetch(url, {
    headers: { 'User-Agent': 'NicheDashboard/1.0' },
    signal: AbortSignal.timeout(15000),
  });

  return new Response(await response.text(), {
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/xml',
      'Cache-Control': 'public, s-maxage=300', // CDN caches 5 min
      'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
    },
  });
}
```

**Key decisions:**
- Server-side proxy is MANDATORY — RSS feeds block browser CORS requests
- Tier system lets you sort by authority (official govt > news outlet > blog)
- 5min CDN cache + 30min client cache = cheap and fast
- Feed cooldown prevents hammering dead feeds

---


## Pattern 2: Panel System (UI Layout)

**What it does:** Organizes feeds into named panels. Each panel = one category of information. Panels are toggleable, reorderable, and variant-aware.

**How World Monitor does it:**
- `src/config/panels.ts` — defines panel name, enabled state, priority per variant
- Grid layout with CSS Grid, panels flow naturally
- Each panel renders its category's feed items as cards
- Panels can be toggled on/off in Settings (persisted to localStorage)

**How to build yours:**

```typescript
// config/panels.ts
export interface PanelConfig {
  name: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  priority: 1 | 2;  // 1=always visible, 2=secondary
}

// Example: Real Estate
export const PANELS: Record<string, PanelConfig> = {
  'market-news':    { name: 'Market News', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'rates':          { name: 'Mortgage Rates', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'policy':         { name: 'Policy & Regulation', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'commercial':     { name: 'Commercial RE', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'residential':    { name: 'Residential', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'ai-brief':       { name: 'AI Daily Brief', enabled: true, priority: 1 },
  'community':      { name: 'Community Buzz', enabled: true, priority: 2 },
  'data':           { name: 'Market Data', enabled: true, priority: 2 },
};
```

**Layout pattern (CSS Grid):**
```css
.dashboard {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(380px, 1fr));
  gap: 16px;
  padding: 16px;
}

.panel {
  background: var(--surface);
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 16px;
  max-height: 600px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}
```

---


## Pattern 3: AI Summary Layer

**What it does:** Takes a batch of headlines from each panel and generates a 2-3 sentence intelligence brief. Falls through providers: local LLM first (free), then cloud (paid), then browser model (slow but free).

**How World Monitor does it:**
- Fallback chain: Ollama (local) -> Groq (fast cloud) -> OpenRouter -> Browser T5 (ONNX in-browser)
- Server-side Redis cache deduplicates summaries across users
- Circuit breaker pattern: if a provider fails 3x, skip it for 60 seconds
- Summaries requested per-panel with geo context

**How to build yours:**

```typescript
// Simplified: call your own /api/summarize endpoint
export async function summarizePanel(headlines: string[], category: string): Promise<string> {
  // Try local Ollama first (free, private)
  try {
    const res = await fetch('http://localhost:11434/api/generate', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({
        model: 'llama3.2',
        prompt: `You are a ${NICHE} intelligence analyst. Summarize these headlines into a 2-sentence brief. Focus on what matters TODAY and what action to take.\n\nHeadlines:\n${headlines.join('\n')}`,
        stream: false,
      }),
    });
    if (res.ok) return (await res.json()).response;
  } catch { /* Ollama not running, fall through */ }

  // Fallback: Claude API via your own serverless function
  const res = await fetch('/api/summarize', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify({ headlines, category }),
  });
  return (await res.json()).summary;
}
```

**Key decisions:**
- Local LLM first = free + private. Users with Ollama get instant summaries at zero cost
- Cloud fallback only fires if local fails
- Cache summaries aggressively (same headlines = same summary)

---
